---
title: "R Notebook"
output: html_notebook
---


```{r}
library(MatchIt)
library(data.table)
library(dplyr)
library(tidyr)
library(plm)
library(fixest)
library(zoo)
library(did)
library(lubridate)
library(googledrive)
library(ggplot2)
library(arrow)
library(stargazer)
library(lmtest)
library(tidyverse)
library(fixest)
library(skimr)
library(plm)
library(modelsummary)
library(MASS)
library(e1071)
```


```{r}
panel = read_parquet('../data/wages_working_data2.parquet')
panel <- panel %>%
  filter(year %in% c(2019, 2020))
panel$log_age <- log(panel$age + 1)
panel
```

```{r}
panel_full = read_parquet('../data/wages_working_data2.parquet')
panel_full$log_age <- log(panel_full$age + 1)
panel_full
```

```{r}
weighted_table <- xtabs(inwgt ~ is_employed, data = panel_full)
print(weighted_table)
```

```{r}
weighted_table <- xtabs(~ gender, data = panel_full)
print(weighted_table)
```

```{r}
weighted_mean <- weighted.mean(panel_full$age, panel_full$inwgt)
print(weighted_mean)

# Weighted standard deviation (unbiased estimator)
weighted_sd <- sqrt(sum(panel_full$inwgt * (panel_full$age - weighted_mean)^2) / 
                    (sum(panel_full$inwgt) - 1))
print(weighted_sd)
```


```{r}
model1 <- feols(
    wages ~ has_disability | idind + year,
    cluster = ~idind,
    data = panel
)
print(summary(model1))

print(confint(model1, level = 0.95))
```

```{r}
etable(model1, 
       tex = TRUE,
       title = "Regression Results",
       label = "tab:results",
       digits = 3)
```

```{r}
library(modelsummary)

# Direct HTML output
modelsummary(model1,
             output = "html",
             title = "Regression Results",
             digits = 3)
```



```{r}
model2 <- feols(
    log(wages + 1) ~ has_disability | idind + year,
    cluster = ~idind,
    data = panel
)
print(summary(model2))

print(confint(model2, level = 0.95))
```

```{r}
ggplot(panel_full, aes(x = factor(has_disability), y = wages)) +
  # Use geom_boxplot to create the plot
  geom_boxplot(alpha = 0.7, fill = "lightblue") + 
  
  # OPTIONAL: Add jitter points to see every single data point 
  # (This helps ensure you aren't missing density information)
  geom_jitter(width = 0.1, alpha = 0.2, color = "darkgrey") + 
  
  # Improve appearance
  labs(title = "Comparison of Score Distribution by Group",
       y = "Score Value",
       x = "Group Category") +
  theme_minimal()
```

```{r}
ggplot(panel, aes(x = factor(has_disability), y = log(wages + 1))) +
  # Use geom_boxplot to create the plot
  geom_boxplot(alpha = 0.7, fill = "lightblue") + 
  
  # OPTIONAL: Add jitter points to see every single data point 
  # (This helps ensure you aren't missing density information)
  geom_jitter(width = 0.1, alpha = 0.2, color = "darkgrey") + 
  
  # Improve appearance
  labs(title = "Comparison of Score Distribution by Group",
       y = "Score Value",
       x = "Group Category") +
  theme_minimal()
```

```{r}
ggplot(panel, aes(x = factor(has_disability), y = age)) +
  # Use geom_boxplot to create the plot
  geom_boxplot(alpha = 0.7, fill = "lightblue") + 
  
  # OPTIONAL: Add jitter points to see every single data point 
  # (This helps ensure you aren't missing density information)
  geom_jitter(width = 0.1, alpha = 0.2, color = "darkgrey") + 
  
  # Improve appearance
  labs(title = "Comparison of Score Distribution by Group",
       y = "Score Value",
       x = "Group Category") +
  theme_minimal()
```

```{r}
ggplot(panel, aes(x = factor(has_disability), y = log(age))) +
  # Use geom_boxplot to create the plot
  geom_boxplot(alpha = 0.7, fill = "lightblue") + 
  
  # OPTIONAL: Add jitter points to see every single data point 
  # (This helps ensure you aren't missing density information)
  geom_jitter(width = 0.1, alpha = 0.2, color = "darkgrey") + 
  
  # Improve appearance
  labs(title = "Comparison of Score Distribution by Group",
       y = "Score Value",
       x = "Group Category") +
  theme_minimal()
```



```{r}
model3 <- feols(
    log(wages + 1) ~ i(period_relevance, has_disability, ref = -1) | idind + year,
    cluster = ~idind,
    data = panel_full
)
print(summary(model3))

print(confint(model3, level = 0.95))
```


```{r}
etable(model3, 
       tex = TRUE,
       title = "Regression Results",
       label = "tab:results",
       digits = 3)
```




```{r}
coefplot(model3)
```


```{r}
model4 <- feols(
    wages ~ i(period_relevance, has_disability, ref = -1) | idind + year,
    cluster = ~idind,
    data = panel_full,
    weights=~inwgt
)
print(summary(model4))

print(confint(model4, level = 0.95))
```

```{r}
etable(model4, 
       tex = TRUE,
       title = "Regression Results",
       label = "tab:results",
       digits = 3)
```



```{r}
coefplot(model4)
```


```{r}
wald_test <- wald(model4, "period_relevance::-2")
print(wald_test)
```

```{r}
coef_df <- broom::tidy(model4, conf.int = TRUE) |>
    filter(str_detect(term, "period_relevance")) |>
    mutate(
        relative_period = as.numeric(str_extract(term, "-?\\d+")),
        type = ifelse(relative_period < 0, "Pre-treatment", "Post-treatment")
    )
coef_df
```


```{r}
broom::tidy(model4, conf.int = TRUE)
```



```{r}
coef_df <- broom::tidy(model4, conf.int = TRUE) |>
    filter(str_detect(term, "period_relevance")) |>
    mutate(
        relative_period = as.numeric(str_extract(term, "-?\\d+")),
        type = ifelse(relative_period < 0, "Pre-treatment", "Post-treatment")
    )

# Add the reference period (k = -1) with zero effect
coef_df <- bind_rows(
    coef_df,
    tibble(
        term = "period_relevance::-1:treatment",
        estimate = 0,
        std.error = 0,
        conf.low = 0,
        conf.high = 0,
        relative_period = -1,
        type = "Pre-treatment"
    )
) |>
    arrange(relative_period)

# Identify max effect for annotation
max_effect_row <- coef_df |> filter(estimate == max(estimate))

# Plot
p_event_study <- ggplot(coef_df, aes(x = relative_period, y = estimate)) +
    # Reference lines
    geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = book_colors$muted, linewidth = 0.8) +
    geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = book_colors$muted, linewidth = 0.8) +
    # Shade pre-treatment region
    annotate("rect",
        xmin = -6.5, xmax = 0,
        ymin = min(coef_df$conf.low) - 0.5, ymax = max(coef_df$conf.high) + 0.5,
        fill = book_colors$light_gray, alpha = 0.5
    ) +
    # Error bars and points
    geom_errorbar(
        aes(ymin = conf.low, ymax = conf.high),
        width = 0.2,
        color = book_colors$primary,
        linewidth = 0.8
    ) +
    geom_point(size = 3, color = book_colors$primary) +
    # Annotations
    annotate("text",
        x = -3.5, y = max(coef_df$conf.high) * 0.7,
        label = "Pre-treatment\n(should be ~0)",
        color = book_colors$muted,
        size = 5.5,
        fontface = "italic"
    ) +
    annotate("text",
        x = 2.5, y = max(coef_df$conf.high) * 0.95,
        label = "Post-treatment\n(treatment effect)",
        color = book_colors$primary,
        size = 5.5,
        fontface = "italic"
    ) +
    labs(
        title = "Event study: effect of OOH campaign on sales over time",
        subtitle = "Reference period: k = -1 (one period before treatment)",
        x = "Periods relative to treatment",
        y = "Estimated effect (millions R$)",
        caption = "Takeaway: No significant pre-trends; effect peaks at k=1, then gradually declines."
    ) +
    scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(-6, 3, 1)) +
    theme_book() +
    theme(
        axis.title = element_text(size = 17)
    )

print(p_event_study)
```

```{r}
coef_df
```



```{r}
coef_df <- broom::tidy(model3, conf.int = TRUE) |>
    filter(str_detect(term, "period_relevance")) |>
    mutate(
        relative_period = as.numeric(str_extract(term, "-?\\d+")),
        type = ifelse(relative_period < -1, "Pre-treatment", "Post-treatment")
    )

# Add the reference period (k = -1) with zero effect
coef_df <- bind_rows(
    coef_df,
    tibble(
        term = "period_relevance::-1:treatment",
        estimate = 0,
        std.error = 0,
        conf.low = 0,
        conf.high = 0,
        relative_period = -1,
        type = "Pre-treatment"
    )
) |>
    arrange(relative_period)

# Identify max effect for annotation
max_effect_row <- coef_df |> filter(estimate == max(estimate))

# Plot
p_event_study <- ggplot(coef_df, aes(x = relative_period, y = estimate)) +
    # Reference lines
    geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = book_colors$muted, linewidth = 0.8) +
    geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = book_colors$muted, linewidth = 0.8) +
    # Shade pre-treatment region
    annotate("rect",
        xmin = -6.5, xmax = 0,
        ymin = min(coef_df$conf.low) - 0.5, ymax = max(coef_df$conf.high) + 0.5,
        fill = book_colors$light_gray, alpha = 0.5
    ) +
    # Error bars and points
    geom_errorbar(
        aes(ymin = conf.low, ymax = conf.high),
        width = 0.2,
        color = book_colors$primary,
        linewidth = 0.8
    ) +
    geom_point(size = 3, color = book_colors$primary) +
    # Annotations
    annotate("text",
        x = -3.5, y = max(coef_df$conf.high) * 0.7,
        label = "Pre-treatment\n(should be ~0)",
        color = book_colors$muted,
        size = 5.5,
        fontface = "italic"
    ) +
    annotate("text",
        x = 2.5, y = max(coef_df$conf.high) * 0.95,
        label = "Post-treatment\n(treatment effect)",
        color = book_colors$primary,
        size = 5.5,
        fontface = "italic"
    ) +
    labs(
        title = "Event plot: effect of pandemic on wages over time",
        subtitle = "Reference period: k = -1 (one period before treatment)",
        x = "Periods relative to treatment",
        y = "Estimated effect (millions R$)",
        caption = "Takeaway: No significant pre-trends; effect peaks at k=1, then gradually declines."
    ) +
    scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(-6, 3, 1)) +
    theme_book() +
    theme(
        axis.title = element_text(size = 17)
    )

print(p_event_study)
```



```{r}
hist(panel_full$wages, main = "Original Data", col = "lightblue")
qqnorm(panel_full$wages, main = "Q-Q Plot (Original)")
qqline(panel_full$wages, col = "red")
```

```{r}
skew_val <- skewness(panel_full$wages)
kurt_val <- kurtosis(panel_full$wages)
```



```{r}
if(abs(skew_val) < 0.5 & abs(kurt_val - 3) < 1) {
  cat("✅ Approximately normal - parametric tests are safe\n")
} else if(abs(skew_val) < 1 & abs(kurt_val - 3) < 2) {
  cat("⚠️ Mild deviation - parametric tests likely still valid\n")
} else {
  cat("❌ Significant deviation - consider robust methods\n")
}
```


```{r}
boxcox_result <- boxcox(lm(panel_full$wages + 1 ~ 1), lambda = seq(-2, 2, 0.1))
boxcox_result
```
```{r}
best_lambda <- boxcox_result$x[which.max(boxcox_result$y)]
print(paste("Optimal lambda:", round(best_lambda, 3)))
```

```{r}
transformed_data <- ((panel_full$wages + 1)^best_lambda - 1) / best_lambda
```


```{r}
transformed_data <- sqrt(panel_full$wages + 1)
dim(transformed_data)
```
```{r}
skew_val <- skewness(transformed_data)
kurt_val <- kurtosis(transformed_data)
```



```{r}
if(abs(skew_val) < 0.5 & abs(kurt_val - 3) < 1) {
  cat("✅ Approximately normal - parametric tests are safe\n")
} else if(abs(skew_val) < 1 & abs(kurt_val - 3) < 2) {
  cat("⚠️ Mild deviation - parametric tests likely still valid\n")
} else {
  cat("❌ Significant deviation - consider robust methods\n")
}
```


```{r}
dim(transformed_data)
```


```{r}
hist(transformed_data, main = "Transformed (λ = 0.263)", col = "lightgreen")
qqnorm(transformed_data, main = "Q-Q Plot (Transformed)")
qqline(transformed_data, col = "blue")
boxplot(transformed_data, main = "Boxplot (Transformed)", col = "lightgreen")
```

```{r}
test_sample <- sample(transformed_data, 5000)

# Test on sample
ks_test <- ks.test(test_sample, "pnorm", 
                   mean = mean(test_sample), 
                   sd = sd(test_sample))

print(ks_test)
```


# PSM

```{r}
names(panel)
```

```{r}
skew_val <- skewness(panel_full$age)
kurt_val <- kurtosis(panel_full$age)
```



```{r}
if(abs(skew_val) < 0.5 & abs(kurt_val - 3) < 1) {
  cat("✅ Approximately normal - parametric tests are safe\n")
} else if(abs(skew_val) < 1 & abs(kurt_val - 3) < 2) {
  cat("⚠️ Mild deviation - parametric tests likely still valid\n")
} else {
  cat("❌ Significant deviation - consider robust methods\n")
}
```


```{r}
#par(mfrow = c(2, 1))
hist(panel_full$age, main = "Original Data", col = "lightblue")
qqnorm(panel_full$age, main = "Q-Q Plot (Original)")
qqline(panel_full$age, col = "red")
```

```{r}
boxcox_result <- boxcox(lm(panel_full$age ~ 1), lambda = seq(-2, 2, 0.1))
boxcox_result
```
```{r}
best_lambda <- boxcox_result$x[which.max(boxcox_result$y)]
print(paste("Optimal lambda:", round(best_lambda, 3)))
```

```{r}
transformed_data <- (panel_full$age^best_lambda - 1) / best_lambda
```

```{r}
hist(transformed_data, main = "Transformed (λ = 0.263)", col = "lightgreen")
qqnorm(transformed_data, main = "Q-Q Plot (Transformed)")
qqline(transformed_data, col = "blue")
boxplot(transformed_data, main = "Boxplot (Transformed)", col = "lightgreen")
```

```{r}
panel_full$age_box_cox = (panel_full$age^best_lambda - 1) / best_lambda
```


```{r}
test_sample <- sample(panel_full$age_box_cox, 5000)

# Test on sample
ks_test <- ks.test(test_sample, "pnorm", 
                   mean = mean(test_sample), 
                   sd = sd(test_sample))

print(ks_test)
```
```{r}
ks_test
```


```{r}
psm_full_probit <- matchit(has_disablity ~ age_box_cox
                 + educ_level_university + is_female + 
                 is_married + gender + is_employed + harmfull_job,
                  data = panel_full ,
                  #method='optimal',
                  method = "full",
                  link = "probit"
                  #distance = "mahalanobis",
                  #distance = "glm",
                  #exact = ~log_age
               )
print(summary(psm_full_probit, un = FALSE))
```


```{r}
psm_full_logit <- matchit(has_disablity ~ age_box_cox
                 + educ_level_university + is_female + 
                 is_married + gender + is_employed + harmfull_job,
                  data = panel_full ,
                  #method='optimal',
                  method = "full",
                  link = "logit"
                  #distance = "mahalanobis",
                  #distance = "glm",
                  #exact = ~log_age
               )
print(summary(psm_full_logit, un = FALSE))
```


```{r}
psm_nn <- matchit(has_disablity ~ age_box_cox
                 + educ_level_university + is_female + 
                 is_married + gender + is_employed + harmfull_job,
                  data = panel_full ,
                  #method='optimal',
                  #method = "full",
                  #link = "logit"
                  #distance = "mahalanobis",
                  #distance = "glm",
                  #exact = ~log_age
               )
print(summary(psm_nn, un = FALSE))
```

```{r}
love.plot(psm_full_probit, stats = c("m", "ks"), poly = 2, abs = TRUE,
          weights = list(nn = psm_nn),
          drop.distance = TRUE, thresholds = c(m = .1),
          var.order = "unadjusted", binary = "std",
          shapes = c("circle filled", "triangle", "square"), 
          colors = c("red", "blue", "darkgreen"),
          sample.names = c("Original", "Full Matching", "NN Matching"),
          position = "bottom")
```

```{r}
love.plot(psm_full_logit, stats = c("m", "ks"), poly = 2, abs = TRUE,
          weights = list(nn = psm_nn),
          drop.distance = TRUE, thresholds = c(m = .1),
          var.order = "unadjusted", binary = "std",
          shapes = c("circle filled", "triangle", "square"), 
          colors = c("red", "blue", "darkgreen"),
          sample.names = c("Original", "Full Matching", "NN Matching"),
          position = "bottom")
```


```{r}
love.plot(psm_full_probit, binary = "std")
```



```{r}
plot(psm_full_probit, type = "density", interactive = FALSE, 
     which.xs = ~ age_box_cox 
                 + educ_level_university + is_female + 
                 is_married + gender + is_employed + harmfull_job
                 )
```



```{r}
plot(summary(psm_full_logit))
```

```{r}
love.plot(psm, binary = "std")
```






```{r}
m.data <- match_data(psm_full_logit)
m.data
```

```{r}
model6 <- feols(
    wages ~ i(period_relevance, has_disability, ref = -1) | idind + year,
    cluster = ~idind,
    weights = ~ weights,
    data = m.data
)
print(summary(model6))

print(confint(model6, level = 0.95))
```

```{r}
m.data <- m.data %>%
  filter(year %in% c(2019, 2020))
model7 <- feols(
    wages ~ has_disability | idind + year,
    cluster = ~idind,
    data = m.data,
    weights = ~ weights,
)
print(summary(model7))

print(confint(model7, level = 0.95))
```

```{r}
modelsummary(model7,
             output = "html",
             title = "Regression Results",
             digits = 3, stars=TRUE)
```

```{r}
modelsummary(model6,
             output = "html",
             title = "Regression Results",
             digits = 3, stars=TRUE)
```



```{r}
coef_df <- broom::tidy(model6, conf.int = TRUE) |>
    filter(str_detect(term, "period_relevance")) |>
    mutate(
        relative_period = as.numeric(str_extract(term, "-?\\d+")),
        type = ifelse(relative_period < -1, "Pre-treatment", "Post-treatment")
    )

# Add the reference period (k = -1) with zero effect
coef_df <- bind_rows(
    coef_df,
    tibble(
        term = "period_relevance::-1:treatment",
        estimate = 0,
        std.error = 0,
        conf.low = 0,
        conf.high = 0,
        relative_period = -1,
        type = "Pre-treatment"
    )
) |>
    arrange(relative_period)

# Identify max effect for annotation
max_effect_row <- coef_df |> filter(estimate == max(estimate))

# Plot
p_event_study <- ggplot(coef_df, aes(x = relative_period, y = estimate)) +
    # Reference lines
    geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = book_colors$muted, linewidth = 0.8) +
    geom_vline(xintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed", color = book_colors$muted, linewidth = 0.8) +
    # Shade pre-treatment region
    annotate("rect",
        xmin = -6.5, xmax = 0,
        ymin = min(coef_df$conf.low) - 0.5, ymax = max(coef_df$conf.high) + 0.5,
        fill = book_colors$light_gray, alpha = 0.5
    ) +
    # Error bars and points
    geom_errorbar(
        aes(ymin = conf.low, ymax = conf.high),
        width = 0.2,
        color = book_colors$primary,
        linewidth = 0.8
    ) +
    geom_point(size = 3, color = book_colors$primary) +
    # Annotations
    annotate("text",
        x = -3.5, y = max(coef_df$conf.high) * 0.7,
        label = "Pre-treatment\n(should be ~0)",
        color = book_colors$muted,
        size = 5.5,
        fontface = "italic"
    ) +
    annotate("text",
        x = 2.5, y = max(coef_df$conf.high) * 0.95,
        label = "Post-treatment\n(treatment effect)",
        color = book_colors$primary,
        size = 5.5,
        fontface = "italic"
    ) +
    labs(
        title = "Event plot: effect of pandemic on wages over time",
        subtitle = "Reference period: k = -1 (one period before treatment)",
        x = "Periods relative to treatment",
        y = "Estimated effect (millions R$)",
        caption = "Takeaway: No significant pre-trends; effect peaks at k=1, then gradually declines."
    ) +
    scale_x_continuous(breaks = seq(-6, 3, 1)) +
    theme_book() +
    theme(
        axis.title = element_text(size = 17)
    )

print(p_event_study)
```


```{r}
write_parquet(m.data, '../data/wages_working_data_psm_full.parquet')
```
